task: Task 1.3 — What the Paper Claims to Improve Over

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: What is the main baseline that Livni et al. 2012 compare against? I know it's multiclass SVM but I want to cite the exact formulation
full_llm_interaction_log.response_summary: The AI confirmed the primary baseline is the Crammer & Singer (2002) multiclass SVM with Frobenius norm regularization on W (Eq. 2 in the paper). It also noted the connection to Xu et al. 2009 for the robust optimization perspective, where the deterministic adversary formulation is discussed in Section 4.
full_llm_interaction_log.what_it_helped_with: Confirmed it's Crammer & Singer 2002 with Frobenius regularization (Eq. 2), and also Xu et al. 2009 for the robust optimization comparison
full_llm_interaction_log.verification_method: Read Section 3.1 and Section 4 (Related Work) to verify

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: In what scenario would the (ℓ₂)∞SVM NOT outperform standard Frobenius SVM? I need to reason about this myself but want a starting point
full_llm_interaction_log.response_summary: The AI pointed out that in the binary case (k=2), Theorem 3.1 shows ℓ₂,∞ reduces to ℓ₂ regularization, making it equivalent to standard SVM. It also mentioned that when classes are already well-separated, the row-wise max regularization doesn't provide additional benefit over uniform Frobenius regularization.
full_llm_interaction_log.what_it_helped_with: Partially — suggested binary case equivalence (which I already knew from the paper). I then extended this reasoning to the glass dataset results in Figure 1 where SVM actually beats RSVM2
full_llm_interaction_log.verification_method: Verified using Figure 1 error rates and binary case analysis in Section 3.1
